hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2/ECL3 and TM5/TM6, while avoiding off-target binding to SSTR3/5 by reducing electrostatic complementarity in non-SSTR2 subtypes.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is that strong ddG values do not guarantee selectivity. The next iteration must prioritize disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions to increase Δmargin and achieve SSTR2 selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are designed to break conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining overall peptide stability and pharmacophore integrity.
